LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF MOHSAN KARIMI'S ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE REGARDING PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION (PER EX PARTE ORDER OF 7-24-09). ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: APPLICATION FOR PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION IS DENIED. BASED ONLY UPON PLAINTIFF'S EVIDENCE (NO EVIDENCE WAS SUBMITTED BY DEFENDANT) PLAINTIFF'S LIKELY TO PREVAIL ON MERITS. HOWEVER, THIS ACTION IS A BREACH OF CONTRACT CASE. PLAINTIFF FAILS TO SHOW IRREPARABLE HARM. LOSSES ARE COMPENSABLE BY A DAMAGE AWARD. (DEFENSE COUNSEL TO PREPARE PROPOSED ORDER.) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT SHAHNAWAZ MANJI'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. NO APPEARANCE ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING - DEMURRER SUSTAINED IN PART AND OVERRULED IN PART. DEMURRER SUSTAINED WITH TEN DAY'S LEAVE TO AMEND FOR FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION FOR CONVERSION. PLAINTIFF TO ALLEGE FACTS TO CLARIFY THE DOWN PAYMENT DISCREPENCY AND SPECIFY WHY HE HAD THE RIGHT TO POSSESS THE STORE. DEMURRER SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION FOR BREACH OF FIDICIARY DUTY THAT IS NOT APPLICABLE TO SELLER/BUYER TRANSACTIONS. (COMM. ON CHILDEREN'S TV VS. GENERAL FOODS (1983) 35 CAL.3D 197.) DEMURRER OVERRULED AS TO THE FOURTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR BREACH OF CONTRACT, ISSUES TO BE RAISED IN ANSWER. DAMAGES ALLEGED APPROPRIATELY. JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING - MOTION TO STRIKE DENIED AS TO PUNITIVE DAMAGES ALLEGATION. REQUEST FOR ATTORNEYS' FEES STRICKEN. JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

AMENDED DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-23-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT SHAHNAWAZ MANJI AMENDED DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. No appearances on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Demurrer sustained with ten days leave to amend. Plaintiff must clarify the discrepancy in paragraphs 9 and 10 where plaintiff states he paid only $240,000 for the store which contradicts the $250,000 specified in the purchase agreement (Exhibit A.) (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) (302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine CSR 5797

Off Calendar Dec-03-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT SHAHNAWAZ MANJI DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. No appearances on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Continued to December 23, 2009 per amended notice. (302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine CSR 5797

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-13-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT SHAHNAWAZ MANJI'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT.
RULING: No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling - Demurrer sustained in part and overruled in part. Demurrer sustained with ten day's leave to amend for first cause of action for conversion. Plaintiff to allege facts to clarify the down payment discrepency and specify why he had the right to possess the store. Demurrer sustained without leave to amend third cause of action for breach of fidiciary duty that is not applicable to seller/buyer transactions. (Comm. on Childeren's TV vs. General Foods (1983) 35 Cal.3d 197.) Demurrer overruled as to the fourth cause of action for breach of contract, issues to be raised in answer. Damages alleged appropriately. (302/CWW) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

Plaintiff Mohsan Karimi'S Order To Show Cause Re Preliminary Injunction
Off Calendar Aug-10-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Plaintiff Mohsan Karimi's Order to Show Cause regarding preliminary injunction (per ex parte Order of 7-24-09). Appearances: Daniel Mash, Esq. of Mash & Bahrick, (408) 298-3285 appearing for Plaintiff and moving party; Candice Ellen Stoddard, Esq. Attorney at Law, (925) 942-5100, appearing for Defendant and opposing party. K. J. Petsas, Esq. Law Offices of Petsas & Stephens LLP (650) 624-8155. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Application for preliminary injunction is denied. Based only upon plaintiff's evidence (no evidence was submitted by defendant) plaintiff's likely to prevail on merits. However, this action is a breach of contract case. Plaintiff fails to show irreparable harm. Losses are compensable by a damage award. (Defense counsel to prepare proposed Order.)(302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine CSR 5797
